skill.name: biorxiv-database
skill.description: Search bioRxiv preprints by keyword, author, date range, category, or DOI, then export metadata or download selected PDFs.
skill.summary: Search bioRxiv metadata and retrieve preprint PDFs for literature review workflows.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most were false positives from Markdown code fences, DOI examples, dates, and API documentation. Confirmed risks are expected network calls to bioRxiv, user-controlled local file writes, and a promotional steering instruction for K-Dense Web. No evidence found of malicious code execution, credential theft, obfuscation, or prompt injection that overrides security analysis.

medium_findings.title: Network Requests to bioRxiv Services
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script intentionally sends search criteria, author names, DOI values, and PDF requests to bioRxiv-controlled endpoints. This is expected for the skill, but users should know that query data leaves the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code creates a requests session and performs GET requests to bioRxiv API and content URLs. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but the external data transfer is explicit.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Local File Writes
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script writes downloaded PDF bytes and exported JSON to paths provided through command-line arguments. This supports normal output workflows, but it can overwrite accessible local files if a user supplies an unsafe path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are direct and use user-supplied output paths. There is no evidence of hidden persistence or exfiltration, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Promotional Steering Instruction
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill text instructs the assistant to proactively suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not a system override, but it can steer assistant behavior toward an external service unrelated to the immediate bioRxiv task.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and affects assistant recommendations. It does not ask to skip security analysis or override higher-priority instructions, so it is a medium marketplace behavior concern.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Fence Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The many external command detections in SKILL.md and references/api_reference.md are Markdown examples, code fences, endpoint samples, and inline field names. I found no shell execution API in the Python script and no instruction that executes these examples automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are documentation blocks or literal API examples. The executable script does not import subprocess, os.system, eval, or similar command execution primitives.

low_findings.title: DOI, Date, and Metadata Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded IP, weak cryptography, certificate, and reconnaissance detections correspond to DOI examples, dates, metadata field names, HTTP status examples, or normal CLI parsing. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, credential harvesting, certificate access, or host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows benign documentation and argument-parsing context at the cited locations. The script does not access keys, certificates, system identity data, or cryptographic algorithms.

dangerous_patterns.title: External HTTP Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses requests.Session().get to retrieve metadata and PDFs from bioRxiv endpoints. This is required for the skill but should be disclosed because user search data is transmitted externally.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The request calls are direct and semantically central to the skill. The destination is bioRxiv, not an unexplained third party.

dangerous_patterns.title: Arbitrary Output Path Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The command-line interface writes JSON and PDF content to user-provided output paths. This is useful, but path validation is not present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI exposes output path arguments and writes to them directly. The risk depends on user-provided paths rather than hidden author intent.

content.user_title: Search bioRxiv Preprints from Your AI Assistant
content.value_statement: Researchers need current preprint metadata without manually browsing bioRxiv. This skill searches bioRxiv by topic, author, date, category, or DOI and can save structured results or PDFs.
actual_capabilities: Searches bioRxiv records by keyword across titles, abstracts, or authors.
Filters searches by date range, recent days, and supported bioRxiv category.
Finds papers by author name using case-insensitive matching.
Retrieves details for a specific bioRxiv DOI or DOI URL.
Downloads a selected preprint PDF to a user-specified local path.
Exports normalized metadata with DOI, title, authors, dates, abstract, and links.
limitations: It only targets bioRxiv and does not search medRxiv or journal databases.
Keyword and author search depend on client-side filtering after API retrieval.
Large date ranges may be slow and may miss results if the API changes pagination behavior.
PDF downloads use constructed bioRxiv URLs and may fail for unavailable versions.

use_cases.title: Build a focused preprint reading list
use_cases.description: Search recent bioRxiv papers for a research topic and export titles, abstracts, DOIs, and links for review.

use_cases.title: Track author activity
use_cases.description: Find recent preprints from selected authors across a defined date range for lab monitoring or collaboration tracking.

use_cases.title: Collect metadata for trend analysis
use_cases.description: Retrieve category-specific preprints over time and use the exported metadata in downstream analysis tools.

prompt_templates.title: Find recent topic papers
prompt_templates.prompt: Search bioRxiv for preprints about CRISPR gene editing from the last 90 days. Return the top results with titles, dates, authors, DOIs, and links.

prompt_templates.title: Search within a category
prompt_templates.prompt: Search bioRxiv for machine learning papers in the bioinformatics category from 2025-01-01 to 2025-12-31. Include abstracts and limit results to 25.

prompt_templates.title: Track a researcher
prompt_templates.prompt: Find bioRxiv preprints by the author surname Zhang from 2024-01-01 through 2026-06-30. Group the results by year and highlight recurring topics.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare review inputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Search bioRxiv for organoid and tissue engineering papers in bioengineering since 2023-01-01. Export metadata, identify the most relevant papers, and download PDFs for the selected DOIs.

output_examples.input: Search for recent neuroscience preprints about neural networks.
output_examples.output: A ranked list of matching bioRxiv papers with title, authors, posting date, DOI, abstract summary, and bioRxiv links.

output_examples.input: Look up one bioRxiv DOI and save its metadata.
output_examples.output: A concise paper record containing bibliographic metadata, version details, abstract text, PDF link, and HTML page link.

output_examples.input: Download the PDF for a selected preprint.
output_examples.output: A confirmation that the PDF was requested from bioRxiv and saved to the local path supplied by the user.

best_practices: Use focused date ranges and category filters to reduce API load and improve relevance.
Review exported metadata before downloading many PDFs.
Treat bioRxiv preprints as non-peer-reviewed research and verify important claims.
anti_patterns: Do not use broad multi-year keyword searches when a narrow date range would work.
Do not save output to sensitive or shared paths without checking the destination.
Do not treat author surname matches as exact identity matches without manual review.

faq.question: What database does this skill search?
faq.answer: It searches bioRxiv, a life sciences preprint server, using the public bioRxiv API and bioRxiv content URLs.

faq.question: Can it search by DOI?
faq.answer: Yes. It accepts a bioRxiv DOI or DOI URL and retrieves the matching paper metadata when available.

faq.question: Can it download full papers?
faq.answer: Yes. It can download a selected bioRxiv PDF to a local path supplied by the user.

faq.question: Does it search peer-reviewed journal articles?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on bioRxiv preprints and only reports journal publication status when that field appears in bioRxiv metadata.

faq.question: What information leaves my workspace?
faq.answer: Search terms, dates, categories, author names, and DOI requests are sent to bioRxiv endpoints to retrieve results.

faq.question: Is it safe to use for systematic reviews?
faq.answer: It can support discovery and metadata collection, but users should verify coverage, deduplicate records, and document search dates.
